attachments.title: Data Driven Rendering: Pipelines | Joren's
attachments.text: Overview Model used in the demo. Data Driven Rendering Series: 1. <https://jorenjoestar.github.io/post/writing_shader_effect_language_1/> 2. <https://jorenjoestar.github.io/post/writing_shader_effect_language_2/> 3. <https://jorenjoestar.github.io/post/writing_shader_effect_language_3/> 4. <https://jorenjoestar.github.io/post/data_driven_rendering_pipeline/> We finally arrived in the Rendering Pipeline realm. Some can write that it is useless, some can hate it. Many have some sort of abstraction for it since ages, and others have to now that new APIs like Vulkan and DX12 have it as an explicit part of their design (finally!

text: <@UE6EFEPTQ> Yeah that does come up sometimes. :confused: The indexeddb error is due to an annoying cache failure, and should be fixable by clearing the site's application data (developers tools -> application -> clear storage -> clear site data). Hopefully we can get rid of the condition in some future update, or at least add an automated recovery.

text: Yup, 2D programming is really an under explored area. But one of the twists of history is that one of the very first programming languages was a 2D language: <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plankalk%C3%BCl>

attachments.title: Plankalkül
attachments.text:
Plankalkül (German pronunciation: [ˈplaːnkalkyːl]) is a programming language designed for engineering purposes by Konrad Zuse  between 1942 and 1945. It was the first high-level programming language to be designed for a computer.
Kalkül is the German term for a formal system—as in Hilbert-Kalkül, the original name for the Hilbert-style deduction system—so Plankalkül refers to a formal system for planning.

text: From the Wiki, I can't see how that's a 2D language. It looks statement-based to me (which is weird because this is well before the era of the terminal — I'd assume this has some sort of mechanical interface. Though maybe that changed as the language was evolved from the 40s to the 70s)

<https://twitter.com/geoffreylitt|@geoffreylitt>: More examples of customizing Hacker News with a spreadsheet:

- show estimated read times (fetched from an API)
- sort longest reads first, prioritize deeper content
- add personal annotations

All in 1 minute, just using formulas. So many possibilities! <https://pbs.twimg.com/ext_tw_video_thumb/1231961954148855808/pu/img/AEC5gUQPhymc_spY.jpg>

text:
Has anyone looked into Ballerina? <https://ballerina.io/>

>  Whenever possible, Ballerina prioritizes programmer convenience & productivity with familiarity, clearer abstractions, and easier concepts over uber system performance.
> In Ballerina, every program is a sequence diagram that illustrates distributed and concurrent interactions automatically. The diagram is the code. The code is the diagram.

attachments.title: Ballerina - Home
attachments.text: A Programming Language for Network Distributed Applications

text: Hello, I’m Mike. I’m a graduate student and my research interest is end-user programming for robots and interactive systems more broadly <https://homes.cs.washington.edu/~mjyc/>. Previously, I mainly worked with block-based visual programming interfaces but recently I’ve been fascinated with the idea of solver-aided programming <https://emina.github.io/rosette/>. So lately, I’ve been thinking about making solver-aided programming more accessible to non-programmer or novice programmers. Nice to meet you all!

text: <@UKP3B2J5D> a lot of tools already use JSON Schema and have helpful tooling around it. It's expanded into a lot of directions as a result of it's many use cases. I would be interested in understanding what it does poorly that you want to accomplish with another language. Could you just specify a subset of JSON Schema and run with that?

text:
Thanks <@UFPPABQ7P>. My current focus is to polish it really well so that it provides an excellent experience for finding and reading code.

Editing experience is bit of more work because then we need to have all the plugins usually people use inside their editors enabled in the bubbles as well. So for the time being, I want to focus on making the reading experience really really good.
